message.thinking:
We need to produce JSON with fields. Summarize the post. The post is by pf_moore, says: "What frontend tools do you expect to support this new interface? I don't expect pip to - pip currently only uses build_wheel and the associated methods, we don't even have a need for build_sdist. Without a frontend using these new APIs, I don't see a good reason for standardising anything. There's also the fact that I've seen almost no demand for building 'non-standard targets' of the form you're describing, but that may simply because I don't work on tools that would see such demand. I note that the examples you found are plugins for hatch, and therefore could easily have been created to satisfy one person's nice use case (I don't know if that's the case, though, so I'm not claiming there's no demand, just that I'm not aware of any)."

He is challenging the need for standardization, evaluating demand, qualifying uncertainty. He is not seeking feedback? He is asking what frontends expect to support. He is also expressing uncertainty about demand. So conversational intent: evaluating, qualifying. Stance: uncertain, evaluating, challenging. Technical burden: high? He mentions pip, build_wheel, build_sdist. He is discussing technical aspects. But the burden is moderate? The parent had high. This post is less technical? He references pip, build_wheel, build_sdist. It's still technical. I'd say medium. Phrase learning potential: high? He uses phrases like "I don't expect pip to", "I don't see a good reason for standardising anything", "I note that the examples you found are plugins for hatch". These are conversational. So high.

Important uncertainty: He says "I don't know if that's the case, though, so I'm not claiming there's no demand, just that I'm not aware of any". So uncertainty about demand. So we can capture that.
